source.title: 2026 Enlistment Bonuses by Branch
source.tier_note: Military.com is a secondary/corroborating source under the master prompt's TOOL POLICY, not a primary .mil source. Official branch bonus tables (DFAS, service recruiting commands) return HTTP 403 to automated retrieval. These figures are therefore SECONDARY-SOURCED and dated, not primary-fetched. They are directional, not authoritative.

hard_caveats: Quoting the article directly: "Your bonus is only guaranteed once it is written into your signed paperwork."
Army bonuses STACK to the $50,000 ceiling — the headline number is what several bonuses ADD UP TO, not a single payment you are handed.
The article states bonuses "fluctuate regularly, sometimes by thousands of dollars from year to year." A figure that was true when this was published may not be true when you sign.
A maximum is not an offer. The headline number is the ceiling for the single most in-demand specialty with the longest contract — it is not what a typical enlistee receives.
Bonuses are usually paid in instalments after you reach the fleet or complete training, not as a lump sum at signing.

The source article itself carries a reader correction flagging its Navy comparison table as potentially inaccurate, and recommends checking navy.com or navycs.com. That warning is carried forward here rather than hidden.
Official Army wording: initial bonus payments are typically made once you have completed initial training. You do not get a cheque at signing.
A bonus under $20,000 is paid as a lump sum AFTER initial training. $20,000 or more is paid HALF after training and the rest in annual instalments. You are not handed a cheque at signing.
Bonuses are TAXED. The quoted number is gross.
If you do not complete the terms, the bonus can be CLAWED BACK — you may have to repay part or all of it. A bonus is payment for service you have not yet performed.

branches.name: U.S. Army
branches.max_note: Per the Army's OWN official page (goarmy.com): "Up to $50K" for full-time recruits COMBINING bonuses for certain jobs. The $50,000 is a stacked ceiling, not a single award.

programs.name: Job Signing Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. For enlisted job openings needing your specific skills and qualifications. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Civilian Acquired Skills Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. For applicants who already hold a skill the Army needs. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Ranger Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. Paid after completing Ranger training and reporting to your first duty station. STACKS with other enlistment bonuses. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Army Reserve Enlistment Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. GUARD/RESERVE — not active-duty compensation. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Quick Ship Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. Requires reporting to Basic Training within 30 days, for in-demand jobs. STACKS with other enlistment bonuses. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Airborne Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. For attending U.S. Army Airborne School. STACKS with other enlistment bonuses. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: College Credit Enlistment Bonus
programs.conditions: Up to. For college or vocational credits — BUT only if you also select a critical MOS that qualifies for a Job Signing Bonus. It does not stand alone. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Reenlistment Bonus
programs.conditions: No figure published. Requires 17+ months continuous active duty, qualification in a critical military skill, and a 3+ year reenlistment. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

programs.name: Medical Professional Bonus (Officer)
programs.conditions: Up to. For health-care professionals serving as commissioned OFFICERS — not an enlisted path. OFFICIAL — goarmy.com.

stacking.rule: Army bonuses STACK, up to the $50,000 active-duty ceiling. This is the part most people miss — the headline figure is what several bonuses add up to, not one payment.
stacking.restriction: The College Credit Enlistment Bonus is "only available if you select a critical MOS that qualifies for a Job Signing Bonus" — it does not stand alone.

branches.payment_terms: Official: "Initial payments for new enlistment bonuses are typically made once you've completed initial training." Timing varies by bonus type and contract. You are not handed a cheque at signing.

branches.name: U.S. Navy
branches.max_note: Stated maximum, for boot-camp entry before October 2026. SEE THE ACCURACY WARNING — the source article's own readers flagged its Navy table.
branches.accuracy_warning: The source article carries a reader correction flagging its Navy comparison table as potentially inaccurate, and recommends verifying against navy.com or navycs.com. Treat these Navy figures with extra caution.

programs.name: Nuclear Field
programs.conditions: Among the highest ASVAB composite requirements in the military.

programs.name: Submarine Electronics Computer Field
programs.conditions: 5-year contract.

programs.name: Sonar Technician Surface
programs.conditions: 5-year contract.

programs.name: Machinist's Mate Submarines
programs.conditions: 5-year contract.

branches.name: U.S. Air Force
branches.no_per_afsc_figures: The source lists eligible bonus FIELDS but no per-AFSC dollar amounts. Do not assume a figure for a specific job.

programs.name: Air National Guard — non-prior service
programs.conditions: Up to. GUARD/RESERVE — not active-duty compensation.

programs.name: Air National Guard — prior service
programs.conditions: Up to. GUARD/RESERVE — not active-duty compensation.

programs.name: Air Force Reserve
programs.conditions: Up to. GUARD/RESERVE — not active-duty compensation.

programs.name: Targeted Investment Plus 2 (QW)
programs.conditions: Requires two extra years of contract.

programs.name: Shipping Bonus (QF)
programs.conditions: Any PEF.

programs.name: Targeted Investment Plus 1 (QV)
programs.conditions: Requires one extra year of contract.

programs.name: Shipping Bonus (QE)
programs.conditions: Any PEF.

branches.name: U.S. Coast Guard
branches.max_note: Per the Coast Guard's own career pages (gocoastguard.com): "Get up to a $75,000 Bonus when you enlist." The $60,000 Critical Rating Affiliation figure is ONE COMPONENT of that ceiling, not the ceiling itself.

conflict.note: RESOLVED. Military.com said $75,000; the Coast Guard hiring-incentives page listed Critical Rating Affiliation at 'up to $60,000', which I initially read as the maximum. The Coast Guard's own rating career pages advertise 'up to a $75,000 Bonus', confirming $75,000 as the overall ceiling and $60,000 as a component of it. Both sources were right; my reading was wrong.

programs.name: Critical Rating Affiliation
programs.conditions: Up to; for qualifying in a needed specialty. OFFICIAL — gocoastguard.com.

programs.name: Higher Education Credits
programs.conditions: Up to; based on college coursework. OFFICIAL — gocoastguard.com.

programs.name: Reserve Assignments
programs.conditions: Up to. GUARD/RESERVE — not active-duty compensation. OFFICIAL — gocoastguard.com.

programs.name: Expedited Shipping
programs.conditions: Up to; for recruits who ship quickly. OFFICIAL — gocoastguard.com.

non_cash_incentives.name: Advanced Paygrade (E-3)
non_cash_incentives.detail: Recruits with college credits, JROTC/ROTC participation, law-enforcement or Sea Explorer background, or scouting achievements may be authorised an immediate advancement to E-3 paygrade after boot camp.

non_cash_incentives.name: Guaranteed Specialty Training
non_cash_incentives.detail: High ASVAB scorers may receive guaranteed placement in a specialised school immediately following basic training.

non_cash_incentives.name: Buddy Program
non_cash_incentives.detail: Friends enlisting together may be placed in the same company at boot camp.

non_cash_incentives.name: Guaranteed First Assignment Area
non_cash_incentives.detail: Certain geographic regions may offer assignment guarantees within specific Coast Guard Districts.

branches.official_caveat: The Coast Guard's own page states these programs 'change frequently' and advises contacting a recruiter for current offerings.

stacking.rule: Coast Guard incentives combine toward the $75,000 ceiling — the headline is a SUM, not one payment.
stacking.source: gocoastguard.com/hiring-incentives + gocoastguard.com rating career pages, retrieved 2026-07-12

branches.name: U.S. Space Force
branches.max_note: NOT COVERED by the source article. No enlistment bonus figure could be found for the Space Force. This is a genuine gap, not an omission — it is not padded with an assumed Air Force figure.

unverified: Army and Coast Guard bonus figures are now OFFICIAL (goarmy.com, gocoastguard.com). Navy, Air Force and Marine Corps remain SECONDARY (Military.com, 2026-06-15) — their official bonus pages could not be retrieved.
The Coast Guard maximum conflict is RESOLVED at $75,000 (official gocoastguard.com career pages). The $60,000 figure is one component (Critical Rating Affiliation), not the ceiling.

Most figures are SECONDARY-SOURCED from Military.com (published 2026-06-15), not primary-fetched. Official service bonus tables return HTTP 403 to automated retrieval. EXCEPTION: the Coast Guard figures are OFFICIAL, taken directly from gocoastguard.com/hiring-incentives (retrieved 2026-07-12).
The Military.com article's own reader correction flags its Navy comparison table as potentially inaccurate. Navy figures carry extra uncertainty.
Air Force per-AFSC bonus amounts are not published in the source — only eligible fields and an overall maximum.
Space Force enlistment bonuses: no data found at all, from either source.
Payment schedules (lump sum vs instalments), tax treatment, and clawback conditions on separation are not covered by either source and were not researched.

MilitaryMoney (militarymoney.com) dollar figures are OLDER and conflict with current official figures; only its structural MECHANICS (payment schedule, tax, recoupment, SRB rules) are used. Its amounts are deliberately not used.
completion_accountability: 6 branches; 5 with figures. Coast Guard is OFFICIALLY sourced (gocoastguard.com); the other four are secondary (Military.com, 2026-06-15). Space Force has no bonus data from either source and is flagged rather than padded. One source conflict (Coast Guard max: $75k secondary vs $60k official) is disclosed, not reconciled.

mechanics.figures_warning: This source's DOLLAR AMOUNTS are older and conflict with current official figures (it self-dates its Navy section to Summer 2022, and lists Air Rescue Swimmer at $24,000 where the current secondary figure is $30,000, and Nuclear Field at $38,000 where the current figure is $75,000). Its dollar amounts are NOT used. Only its MECHANICS — how bonuses are paid, taxed and clawed back — are used, because those are structural and stable.

how_it_is_paid.under_20k: A bonus under $20,000 is normally paid as a LUMP SUM — but only after you complete initial training. Not at signing.
how_it_is_paid.over_20k: A bonus of $20,000 or more is normally paid HALF up front (after initial training) and the REST IN ANNUAL INSTALMENTS on your enlistment anniversary. You must remain in good standing to receive each anniversary payment.
how_it_is_paid.plain: The headline number is almost never a cheque you receive at signing. Budget accordingly.

mechanics.tax: Bonuses ARE taxable and are subject to withholding. The number you are quoted is a GROSS figure — what lands in your account is less.

recoupment.plain: If you do not complete the terms of the bonus agreement, you may have to REPAY part or all of it.
recoupment.detail: This is called recoupment or clawback. It applies if you separate early, fail to qualify or maintain the skill, or lose the qualification the bonus was paid for. Repayment also creates a tax mess — if the repayment falls in a different tax year from the payment, you may need an amended return.
recoupment.why_it_matters: A bonus is not a gift. It is payment for a term of service you have not yet performed. If you leave early, the money can be taken back.

mechanics.adjusted_regularly: Enlistment and reenlistment bonuses are routinely adjusted as recruiting needs change. Any figure — including on this site — can be out of date by the time you sign.

reenlistment.name: Selective Reenlistment / Retention Bonus (SRB)
eligibility: 17+ months of continuous active duty (and not more than 14 years).
Qualified in a critical military skill.
Reenlist or extend for a minimum of 3 years.
reenlistment.maximum: Up to $25,000 per year of reenlistment, topping out at $100,000 for a four-year reenlistment. A member may qualify for up to TWO SRBs in a career, for a maximum of $200,000 total.
reenlistment.payment: Typically 50% as a lump sum at reenlistment, with the remainder in equal annual instalments.
reenlistment.note: This is a MID-CAREER decision, not an entry one. It is here because the reenlistment fork — usually at the end of your first term — is where the money actually gets large, and almost nobody tells a 17-year-old that.
